title: Plectronia fililoba
taxonomy: Plectronia fililoba is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The genus Plectronia belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The Rubiaceae family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Plectronia fililoba, like other members of the family, is an evergreen or semi-evergreen shrub or small tree. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia.
uses: Plectronia fililoba is reported to have various medicinal uses, including the treatment of fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Its leaves are used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain. In some cultures, Plectronia fililoba is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide relief from symptoms of colds and flu, and also help to soothe digestive issues.
